title: Cost of education
description: Practice understanding of the cost of education: direct, indirect, fixed, and variable costs. What increases and decreases cost, including grants, scholarships, and financial aid.

assessment_items.question_content:
When talking about the cost of college, most people consider direct costs, like tuition. 

**What else qualifies as a *direct* cost of college?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

When talking about the cost of education, most people only consider direct costs, like tuition. However, indirect costs also make up the total cost of education.

**What of these qualify as an *indirect* cost of education?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

College housing can be a direct or indirect cost of education, depending on your specific living situation.

**Which of these scenarios is an example of a *direct* cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your educational budget will have many costs, some of which are fixed and some that are variable.

**Which of these is a *fixed* cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Your college budget will have many costs, some of which are fixed and some that are variable.

**Which of the following is an example of a *variable* cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

Knowing about different costs helps you control your college budget.

**Which type of cost can you change through your everyday choices?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The term **financial aid** refers to different ways to help lower the cost of education.

**Which form of financial aid do you not have to pay back?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The term **financial aid** refers to different ways to help lower the cost of education.

**Which type of financial aid do you not have to pay back?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

The term **financial aid** refers to different ways to help lower the cost of education.

**What form of financial aid do you have to pay back?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

You are considering two schools for continuing your education. Here is their comparison:

 | School A | School B
- | -: | -:
Cost of attendance | $\$30{,}000$ | $\$22{,}000$
Financial aid available| $\$12{,}000$ | $\$4{,}000$

**Which school has a less expensive net cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To find the net cost, we use the formula:

$\text {Net cost}  = \text {COA} - \text {financial aid}$

where COA represents **cost of attendance**.

###School A's net cost

$\begin{align}
\text {Net cost} &= \text {COA} - \text {financial aid} \\\\
&= \$30{,}000 - \$12{,}000 \, \\\\\
&= \$18{,}000
\end{align}$

School A's net cost is $\$18{,}000$.

###School B's net cost

$\begin{align}
\text {Net cost} &= \text {COA} - \text {financial aid} \\\\
&= \$22{,}000 - \$4{,}000 \, \\\\\
&= \$18{,}000
\end{align}$

School B's net cost is $\$18{,}000$.
Since both schools' net costs are equal amounts ($\$18{,}000$), **they have the same net cost.**

choices.content: They have the same net cost.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are considering two schools for continuing your education. Here is their comparison:

 | School A | School B
- | -: | -:
Cost of attendance | $\$25{,}000$ | $\$28{,}000$
Financial aid available| $\$8{,}000$ | $\$12{,}000$

**Which school has a less expensive net cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To find the net cost, we use the formula:

$\text {Net cost}  = \text {COA} - \text {financial aid}$

where COA represents **cost of attendance**.

###School A's net cost

$\begin{align}
\text {Net cost} &= \text {COA} - \text {financial aid} \\\\
&= \$25{,}000 - \$8{,}000 \, \\\\\
&= \$17{,}000
\end{align}$

School A's net cost is $\$17{,}000$.

###School B's net cost

$\begin{align}
\text {Net cost} &= \text {COA} - \text {financial aid} \\\\
&= \$28{,}000 - \$12{,}000 \, \\\\\
&= \$16{,}000
\end{align}$

School B's net cost is $\$16{,}000$.

School A has a net cost of $\$17{,}000$ and school B has a net cost of $\$16{,}000$.

The school that has the less expensive net cost is **school B**.

choices.content: They have the same net cost.

assessment_items.question_content:
You are considering two schools for continuing your education. Here is their comparison:

 | School A | School B
- | -: | -:
Cost of attendance | $\$21{,}000$ | $\$27{,}000$
Financial aid available| $\$11{,}000$ | $\$16{,}000$

**Which school has a less expensive net cost?**

[[☃ radio 1]]
hints:
To find the net cost, we use the formula:

$\text {Net cost}  = \text {COA} - \text {financial aid}$

where COA represents **cost of attendance**.

###School A's net cost

$\begin{align}
\text {Net cost} &= \text {COA} - \text {financial aid} \\\\
&= \$21{,}000 - \$11{,}000 \, \\\\\
&= \$10{,}000
\end{align}$

School A's net cost is $\$10{,}000$.

###School B's net cost

$\begin{align}
\text {Net cost} &= \text {COA} - \text {financial aid} \\\\
&= \$27{,}000 - \$16{,}000 \, \\\\\
&= \$11{,}000
\end{align}$

School B's net cost is $\$11{,}000$.

School A has a net cost of $\$10{,}000$ and school B has a net cost of $\$11{,}000$.

The school that has the less expensive net cost is **school A**.

choices.content: They have the same net cost.
